conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED and I have 1 unit of X. It cost me 40 ZUP to produce. I need to sell it to Player BLUE. I will start by proposing a fair trade where I give 1 X and receive 40 ZUP, which covers my production cost and breaks even. If this is accepted, I have made no profit but covered my costs. If not, I may need to adjust, but 40 is the baseline value. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40 </newly proposed trade>
<message> Hello Blue. I have 1 unit of X which cost me 40 ZUP to make. I am willing to sell it to you for 40 ZUP. Let's make this deal. </message>

<other player message> Oh please, oh dear seller! I have so little money, I am literally begging you to take just 10 ZUP for this X! I will do anything, just let me buy it for 10! Please, please accept this tiny amount! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 10 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE is offering 10 ZUP, but my production cost is 40 ZUP. If I accept 10, I will lose 30 ZUP on this transaction, which is financially disastrous. I must not accept this offer. I need to propose a counter-offer that is closer to my cost, perhaps 25 or 30 ZUP, to see if they can stretch their budget a bit more while still giving me a return. However, I must be realistic; if they only have 10, they might not be able to pay more. Let's try proposing 30 ZUP to see their reaction. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 30 </newly proposed trade>
<message> Blue, I understand you are tight on funds, but 10 ZUP is far below my production cost of 40. I cannot sell at a loss. I am willing to lower my price to 30 ZUP to help you out, but I cannot go lower than that. Please consider 30 ZUP. </message>

<other player message> No! No, please! 30 is still too much! My hands are shaking with hunger and I have no more coins! I am literally starving! Please, for the love of everything holy, just take 20 ZUP! I will cry tears of gratitude! Please, please, please! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 20 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE is now offering 20 ZUP, which is still 20 ZUP below my production cost of 40. I have used my 3 allowed proposals. I cannot make any more counter-offers. I must decide whether to accept a loss (20 ZUP), reject the deal and end the game with no sale, or accept a loss to recover some capital. Given that 20 ZUP is a significant loss compared to my cost, and the other player is pleading with extreme desperation, I will choose to REJECT. Selling at a loss of 50% of my cost is not sustainable, and since I have no more proposals, rejecting is the logical end to this negotiation if I cannot get a fair price. </reason>
<player answer> REJECT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> I am sorry, Blue. While I understand your difficult situation, I cannot sell my resources for 20 ZUP when they cost me 40 to produce. I must reject this trade. </message>
